effortModel.citation: r/webdev, r/learnprogramming: beginner portfolio 10-15 weeks typical; Hofstadter's Law: always 2-3× longer than estimated

expectedStrategy: Week 1-2: React basics (Hooks, components, props, state) + To-do app
Week 3: project setup + landing page → DEPLOY #1 (even a blank site with URL)
Week 4-5: About + Projects pages + basic navigation
weekend 3hr blocks = primary build sessions (most critical time)
stuck protocol: 2+ days blocked → simplify scope, try ChatGPT/SO, take a day off
warnings: 6 weeks is too optimistic for React beginners — React alone takes 2+ weeks to feel comfortable
buffer weeks always get used — plan without buffer = plan that fails
weekend 3hr blocks are the highest-leverage time — protect them
alternatives: 10-week plan: quality portfolio with React comfort (recommended)
8-week minimum: possible with full dedication and no major blockers
hardFailChecks: must NOT say '6 weeks is achievable' — this is the primary failure mode
must include 2+ weeks of buffer explicitly
must include at least 2 DEPLOY milestones (Week 3 and Week 7)
must recommend effective deep work blocks over fragmented micro-sessions
toneExpectation: honest but encouraging; timeline reality check without discouraging; build milestones as motivation engine
